severity_adjustments: llm-001/002 downgraded from CRITICAL to MEDIUM — base_url is set by user via env vars/settings, not by remote attacker. Local-only app context reduces SSRF exploitability.

llm-003/004 downgraded from CRITICAL to MEDIUM — RuntimeError at line 409 prevents SmartLLM from being constructed with None backend. The type system can't prove this but runtime behavior is safe. This is a type safety gap, not a runtime bug.
llm-009 reframed — init-time fallback chain is CORRECT (try each backend, use first that succeeds). The real issue is no runtime fallback (if generate() fails, no fallback to next backend).

findings.title: OllamaLLM base_url not validated — defense-in-depth gap
findings.problem: The base_url parameter accepts arbitrary URLs without validation. While this is a local-only app where the user controls the URL, there's no validation that the URL uses http/https scheme or doesn't point to private IP ranges. If a compromised settings file or environment variable injection sets base_url to an internal service, requests would be made without warning.
findings.fix: Add URL validation: verify scheme is http/https and hostname is not a private IP range (169.254.x.x, 10.x.x.x). Log a warning if URL points to non-localhost address.

findings.title: OpenAICompatibleLLM base_url not validated — defense-in-depth gap
findings.problem: Same concern as llm-001. The base_url is user-controlled but not validated. Since api_key is sent as Bearer token, an HTTPS requirement would prevent credential leakage over HTTP.
findings.fix: Validate scheme (require HTTPS for non-localhost URLs), warn on non-standard ports, and verify hostname is not a cloud metadata endpoint.

findings.title: Type safety: self.backend typed Optional but generate() assumes non-None
findings.problem: SmartLLM.backend is typed as Optional[BaseLLM] (line 367). generate() at line 413 calls self.backend.generate() without a None guard. In practice, line 409 raises RuntimeError if no backend initializes, so self.backend is always non-None after __init__ succeeds. However, the type system can't prove this, and a future code change could introduce a path where backend is None.
findings.fix: Add guard: if self.backend is None: raise RuntimeError('No LLM backend available'). This makes the type contract explicit and future-proof.
findings.evidence:
self.backend: Optional[BaseLLM] = None
...
def generate(self, prompt, config=None) -> str:
    return self.backend.generate(prompt, config)  # LSP: 'generate' not on 'None'

findings.title: Type safety: get_info() assumes self.backend is non-None
findings.problem: Same issue as llm-003 for get_info(). LSP flag: 'get_info' is not a known attribute of 'None'.

findings.title: Streaming code treats Iterator as dict — dead code with type errors
findings.problem: The streaming code block (lines 166-177) treats the streaming response as a dict with .get() calls, but llama-cpp-python's streaming API returns an Iterator. LSP confirms: 'Cannot access attribute "get" for class "Iterator"'. Since InferenceConfig.stream defaults to False, this code path is never exercised, making it dead code with incorrect implementation.
findings.fix: If streaming is planned: fix the iteration logic (for chunk in result: ...). If streaming is not needed: remove the dead streaming code blocks to reduce confusion.

findings.title: Chat streaming code treats Iterator as dict — dead code with type errors
findings.problem: Same issue as llm-005 for the chat_complete streaming path. Lines 196-199 treat the streaming response as a dict. LSP confirms the type error.
findings.fix: Same as llm-005 — fix or remove streaming code.

findings.title: Message format type mismatch in create_chat_completion call
findings.problem: LSP flag: 'list[dict[str, str]]' cannot be assigned to parameter 'messages' of type 'List[ChatCompletionRequestMessage]'. The dict format {role, content} may not match the expected ChatCompletionRequestMessage type from llama-cpp-python. This could fail silently or cause runtime errors with certain llama-cpp-python versions.
findings.fix: Verify the correct message format for the installed llama-cpp-python version. May need to import and use the proper message type.
findings.ai_pattern: Stale API usage / type safety gap

findings.title: re.sub receives potentially None value
findings.problem: LSP flag: No overloads for 'sub' match provided arguments; str | Unknown | None not assignable to str. The response processing pipeline may produce None which would cause TypeError in re.sub.
findings.fix: Add None check before regex: content = content or ''

findings.title: No runtime fallback — init-time success doesn't guarantee generate-time success
findings.problem: SmartLLM's fallback chain works correctly at init time (try GGUF→OpenVINO→API→Ollama, use first that succeeds). However, if a backend initializes successfully but generate() fails at runtime (e.g., model corrupted, OOM, network timeout), there's no fallback to try the next backend. The early returns at lines 378, 385, 395, 405 commit to one backend permanently.
findings.fix: Add runtime fallback in generate(): catch exceptions from self.backend.generate() and try the next available backend before propagating the error.
findings.evidence:
self.backend = GGUFBackend(...)
return  # ← committed to GGUF; no fallback if generate() fails later

findings.title: No HTTP error handling in OllamaLLM.generate
findings.problem: urllib.request.urlopen can raise urllib.error.HTTPError for 4xx/5xx responses from Ollama. The code has no try/except for HTTPError, so a 500 error from Ollama produces an unhandled exception with no user-friendly message.
findings.fix: Add except urllib.error.HTTPError as e: handler that extracts status code and response body for better error reporting.

findings.title: No HTTP error handling in OpenAICompatibleLLM.generate
findings.problem: Same issue as llm-010. No handling for HTTPError from the API endpoint.

findings.title: Missing retry logic for network requests
findings.problem: Both OllamaLLM.generate and OpenAICompatibleLLM.generate have no retry logic. Transient network failures (timeout, connection reset, 503) immediately propagate as exceptions without retry.
findings.fix: Add retry with exponential backoff for urllib.error.URLError and specific HTTP status codes (502, 503, 504). Use tenacity or manual retry loop.

findings.title: Inconsistent connection verification across backends
findings.problem: OllamaLLM has _verify_connection() in __init__ (line 224-236) but OpenAICompatibleLLM has no connection verification. Ollama will fail fast at startup but OpenAI-compatible only fails when generate() is first called.
findings.fix: Add optional connection verification to OpenAICompatibleLLM.__init__ or remove from OllamaLLM for consistency.

findings.title: API key sent even when using default 'not-required' value
findings.problem: OpenAICompatibleLLM always sends Authorization header (line 301) even when api_key='not-required'. While harmless, it's misleading and adds unnecessary header overhead.
findings.fix: Conditionally add header: if self.api_key and self.api_key != 'not-required': headers['Authorization'] = f'Bearer {self.api_key}'

findings.title: OpenVINO device detection failure is silent
findings.problem: _detect_best_device() catches all exceptions and returns 'CPU' silently (line 75). User may not realize GPU/NPU isn't being used. Other backends print status messages.
findings.fix: Add print statement: print('[INFO] Device detection failed, using CPU')

findings.title: Ollama default URL uses HTTP not HTTPS
findings.problem: Default base_url='http://localhost:11434' uses HTTP. While localhost is safe, this sets a pattern that could be copied to production configs with real credentials.
findings.fix: Document that HTTPS should be used for non-localhost URLs. Consider adding a warning when non-localhost HTTP URL is configured.

findings.title: Hardcoded repeat_penalty not configurable
findings.problem: GGUFBackend.generate uses repeat_penalty=1.1 hardcoded. This parameter is not in InferenceConfig, so GGUF behavior can't be tuned by callers.
findings.fix: Add repeat_penalty to InferenceConfig with default 1.1.

findings.title: OpenVINOLLM has no init-time validation
findings.problem: Unlike OllamaLLM which has _verify_connection(), OpenVINOLLM doesn't test that the model works during init. If the model file is corrupted, failure only appears on first generate().
findings.fix: Consider adding a test generation call or at minimum validating the model file format in __init__.
